fields.name: Manoj Kumar Selvaraj
fields.title: Cloud Platform Engineer | DevOps Engineer
fields.tagline: Building and modernizing enterprise DevOps platforms on AWS with reliability-first architecture.
fields.about_section_intro: Enterprise DevOps, AWS platform engineering, and AI system evaluation in production contexts.
fields.projects_section_intro: Large-scale migrations, automation frameworks, and full-stack systems built in production.
fields.blog_section_intro: Thoughts on AI, DevOps, cloud infrastructure and engineering.
fields.contact_section_intro: Send a message and I'll get back to you.
fields.bio:
Cloud and DevOps engineer focused on building, scaling, and modernizing enterprise DevOps platforms on AWS.

Strong expertise across Terraform, Kubernetes, CI/CD ecosystems, IAM, and cloud networking, with hands-on delivery in large migrations, Python automation frameworks, and high-availability platform operations.

Also focused on AI system evaluation, prompt engineering, and LLM agent benchmarking, combining infrastructure depth with rigorous validation of AI-driven systems.
fields.bio_extended:
Core outcomes: platform reliability, migration safety, reusable automation, and measurable engineering velocity gains.
This portfolio captures architecture practices and tooling decisions used across enterprise systems.
fields.applications_section_body: Hands-on scope includes platform engineering for CI/CD ecosystems, Kubernetes operations, Terraform automation, cloud security hardening, and reliability engineering at enterprise scale.
fields.infra_section_body: Reference patterns include multi-account AWS architecture, private networking, EKS runtime platforms, policy-driven IAM, and automated delivery frameworks with observability guardrails.

fields.name: AWS (EC2, EKS, S3, IAM, KMS, VPC, CloudFront, Route53)
Terraform Platform Modernization (TFE -> TFC)
fields.role:
Infrastructure provisioning control plane for multi-account AWS environments.
Standardized reusable modules and policy guardrails.
fields.setup:
Terraform Cloud workspaces mapped by environment and account.
VCS-driven runs with variable sets, workspace policies, and state governance.
fields.usage:
Pipeline or PR change triggers plan/apply workflows.
Automations validate drift, deprecated resources, and workspace readiness before migration.
fields.communication:
Integrates with GitHub Enterprise, IAM roles, and cloud APIs.
Status feedback loops into CI/CD and operations dashboards.
fields.tradeoffs:
Centralized control improves consistency but requires strict workspace hygiene.
Migration pace depends on state quality and module standardization depth.

fields.name: Jenkins / CloudBees CI Runtime Platform
fields.role: Primary CI execution backbone for high-volume enterprise delivery pipelines.
fields.setup:
CloudBees CI controllers with resilient storage and backup strategy.
Shared libraries and standardized agent templates for team-level consistency.
fields.usage:
Build/test/deploy workflows execute per repository and release process.
Operational playbooks handle runner capacity, failures, and upgrades.
fields.communication:
Connects to GitHub Enterprise, Artifactory, SonarQube, and deployment tools.
Feeds logs/metrics to monitoring and incident workflows.
fields.tradeoffs:
Highly flexible platform with operational overhead at large scale.
Standardization reduces team variance but can slow custom pipeline changes.

fields.name: GitHub Actions ARC on EKS
fields.role: Elastic runner platform for distributed GitHub Actions workloads.
fields.setup:
ARC runners deployed across EKS clusters in multiple AWS accounts.
OIDC-based role assumption used for short-lived AWS access.
fields.usage:
Workflows schedule onto ARC runners based on labels and concurrency.
Autoscaling responds to queue load while maintaining runner isolation.
fields.communication:
GitHub Actions control plane triggers jobs; runners access AWS services via IAM roles.
Monitoring integrates with CloudWatch alarms and platform alerts.
fields.tradeoffs:
Great elasticity and security posture; requires Kubernetes operational maturity.
Cross-account governance and upgrade cadence are ongoing responsibilities.

fields.name: Enterprise Code Quality and Artifact Flow
fields.role: Quality gates and artifact governance across CI/CD pipelines.
fields.setup:
SonarQube and JFrog integrated into Jenkins/Harness workflows.
Plugin and runtime compatibility baselines managed during upgrades.
fields.usage:
Builds push binaries/images after scan and policy checks pass.
Quality metrics and failure signals block unsafe promotions.
fields.communication:
Bi-directional integration with CI engines, scanners, and artifact repositories.
Alerts and dashboards feed operational incident workflows.
fields.tradeoffs:
Improves release confidence but increases pipeline dependency chain.
Version upgrades need pre-validation to avoid ecosystem breakage.

fields.name: Full Stack Business Management Platform (Personal Project)
fields.role: Business workflow platform for employee, inventory, and billing operations.
fields.setup:
React frontend on S3 + CloudFront; Django backend on EC2 behind ALB.
Provisioned via Terraform with IAM/networking controls.
fields.usage:
Users interact via React SPA; Django APIs process business operations.
CI/CD automates frontend and backend deployments.
fields.communication:
Frontend communicates with Django APIs over HTTPS.
Backend integrates with MySQL host through secure role-based access patterns.
fields.tradeoffs:
Simple architecture for SMB scale with clear ownership boundaries.
Requires disciplined infra automation and release validation for long-term maintainability.

fields.title: Full Stack Business Management Platform
fields.description: Full-stack web application using React.js (S3 + CloudFront) and Django (EC2 + ALB) to manage employee records, inventory, billing, and operational workflows for a small business. Complete AWS infrastructure provisioned via Terraform OSS, CI/CD via CodePipeline + CodeBuild + CodeDeploy. Included a Python framework to auto-generate infrastructure architecture diagrams from live AWS resources.

fields.title: Terraform Enterprise to Cloud Migration
fields.description: Migrated 1,000+ Terraform Enterprise workspaces to Terraform Cloud, resolving state drift, deprecated resources, sensitive variable handling, VCS integration issues, and outdated Terraform versions. Built Python automation to orchestrate workspace migration and validation, reducing overall migration effort by 50%.

fields.title: Jenkins CJP to CloudBees CI Migration
fields.description: Led enterprise Jenkins migration from traditional CJP to CloudBees CI, migrating 15,000+ pipelines and ~500 GB of Jenkins home data. Designed a highly available Jenkins architecture with resilient storage and backup strategies. Cleaned up unused jobs reclaiming over 100 GB of storage.

fields.title: Mainframe Scheduler Migration (ESP to StoneBranch)
fields.description: Developed a Python-based automation tool that migrated 3,000 mainframe jobs from ESP to StoneBranch, ensuring 100% accuracy through data validation scripts. Integrated mainframe batch processes with AWS-based monitoring and file transfer automation, initiating modernization initiatives.

fields.title: GitHub Actions ARC Runners on EKS
fields.description: Deployed GitHub Actions Actions Runner Controller (ARC) runners in EKS clusters across 5 AWS accounts, enabling 500+ workflows per day with zero service interruption. Implemented OIDC-based authentication for GitHub Actions, eliminating long-lived IAM credentials and enabling secure short-lived role-based access to AWS.

fields.title: SonarQube Upgrade (9.x → 2025.1.4)
fields.description: Upgraded SonarQube through a major version jump and validated quality gates across multiple tech stacks in Jenkins and Harness. Tested rollout end-to-end on Jenkins running on EC2 and on EKS, aligning plugins, JDK/scanners, and pipeline templates for low-downtime execution.

fields.title: Manual AI Code Evaluation (Large Python Repositories)
fields.description: Manually evaluated AI-generated code changes for large Python codebases: correctness, edge cases, test impact, style consistency, and safety. Produced structured scoring rubrics and repeatable review workflows.

fields.title: IAM User Decommissioning Automation
fields.description: Built an automated decommissioning workflow for IAM users across multiple AWS accounts: access validation, key/token cleanup, policy detachment, and audit-ready reporting with safe rollback checks.

fields.title: Kubernetes Cluster Upgrade Program
fields.description: Planned and executed Kubernetes/EKS version upgrades (control plane, node groups, and core add-ons) with phased rollouts, workload validation, and rollback playbooks across a large cluster fleet.

fields.title: AI Agent Benchmarking Framework
fields.description: Built reproducible evaluation workflows for AI coding agents: adversarial task design, Docker-based environments, deterministic oracle solutions, and automated verification suites to validate code correctness and multi-step tool use.
fields.long_description:
Designed end-to-end evaluation tasks that mirror real software engineering work: multi-step changes in real repositories, strict acceptance criteria, and deterministic validation.

Created benchmark task environments that include Docker images, oracle implementations, and automated verification suites so results are reproducible across runs.

Focused on measuring correctness, robustness, and maintainability of agent-produced code, with clear rubrics and iterative prompt refinement based on observed failure modes.

fields.description: Owned and modernized enterprise DevOps platforms across AWS, Kubernetes, and CI/CD ecosystems.
highlights: Owned enterprise DevOps platforms: Jenkins, Harness, Terraform Enterprise/Cloud, GitHub Enterprise, SonarQube, JFrog, and UDeploy.
Migrated 1000+ Terraform Enterprise workspaces to Terraform Cloud with automated validation workflows.
Built Python automation reducing migration effort by ~50%.
Led Jenkins migration from CJP to CloudBees CI, moving 15000+ pipelines and ~500 GB Jenkins home data.
Implemented OIDC authentication for GitHub Actions to remove long-lived IAM credentials.
Scaled ARC runners in EKS across 5 AWS accounts to support 500+ workflows/day.
Led EKS upgrades across 30+ AWS accounts with workload validation and add-on compatibility checks.

fields.description: Supported mission-critical mainframe disbursement systems and drove modernization by migrating scheduler workloads to AWS-integrated automation.
highlights: Supported mission-critical mainframe disbursement systems with 99.99% SLA.
Delivered production changes and fixes to meet regulatory and business requirements.
Developed a Python-based automation tool migrating 3,000 mainframe jobs from ESP to StoneBranch with 100% accuracy.
Integrated mainframe batch processes with AWS-based monitoring and file transfer automation.

fields.description: Work on structured evaluation and benchmarking of AI coding agents with a focus on reproducible engineering tasks, deterministic validation, and clear scoring rubrics.
highlights: SECTION: AI Agent Code Evaluation in Open Source Repositories
Selected real pull requests from active open-source Python repositories and converted them into structured agent tasks.
Designed prompts that describe the change requirements and repository context so that AI agents can implement the modification.
Evaluated multiple agent-generated implementations, comparing correctness, reasoning quality, maintainability, and edge-case handling.
Documented strengths and weaknesses of each implementation including missed requirements, incorrect assumptions, and incomplete fixes.
Iteratively refined prompts based on agent mistakes to improve instruction clarity and coverage of edge cases.
Conducted multi-turn evaluation cycles (typically three or more iterations) until the agent successfully implemented the required change.
Verified that the final implementation satisfies the repository’s functional requirements and passes relevant tests.
SECTION: AI Benchmark Task Engineering (TerminalBench)
Designed adversarial engineering tasks for benchmarking AI coding agents using the TerminalBench framework.
Built complete task environments including Docker containers, deterministic oracle solutions, and automated verification tests.
Authored structured task prompts (instruction.md) describing real engineering problems in a concise and human-authentic style.
Implemented deterministic oracle solutions to demonstrate correct behavior and provide a baseline for agent evaluation.
Developed automated verification scripts and pytest-based validation suites to ensure reproducible task outcomes.
Defined scoring rubrics to evaluate agent behavior, correctness, safety, and problem-solving strategies.
Ensured tasks meet difficulty thresholds by evaluating agent success rates across multiple runs.
Created environments requiring multi-step reasoning, debugging, and terminal interaction rather than single-command solutions.
Validated tasks using automated CI checks and agent evaluation runs before submission to the benchmark platform.

fields.description: Evaluated and benchmarked LLM systems and coding agents through adversarial tasks and reproducible validation environments.
highlights: Reviewed and rated LLM outputs for correctness, reasoning quality, and instruction adherence.
Designed prompts and adversarial coding tasks to test edge-case handling and tool use.
Built validation test suites and reference fixes to assess completion accuracy.

fields.description: Supported mission-critical mainframe disbursement systems and drove scheduler migration automation.
highlights: Maintained 99.99% SLA for business-critical mainframe systems.
Developed Python automation to migrate 3000 jobs from ESP to StoneBranch with validation checks.
Integrated batch processes with AWS-based monitoring and file transfer automation.
